edges.description: Medium-artery inflammation can narrow, thrombose, or weaken vessels, leading to tissue ischemia and organ-specific PAN manifestations.
Immune-mediated inflammatory pathways sustain necrotizing medium-artery injury in idiopathic PAN.

nodes.description: Vascular lesions in medium arteries can produce single-organ disease, cutaneous PAN, or severe systemic disease with polyvisceral failure.

evidence.snippet: The spectrum of disease ranges from involving a single organ to polyvisceral failure.

nodes.description: In primary or idiopathic PAN, the specific initiating mechanism remains uncertain, but clinical response to immunosuppression supports an immune contribution to vessel-wall inflammation.

evidence.snippet: The pathogenesis of "idiopathic PAN" remains enigmatic, although the clinical responses to immunosuppressive therapy support the concept that immunological mechanisms play an active pathogenic role.

nodes.description: Transmural inflammatory injury affects medium-sized muscular arteries, damaging the vessel wall and promoting fibrinoid necrosis, aneurysm, stenosis, thrombosis, or other ischemia-producing vascular lesions.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical Approach to Diagnosis and Therapy of Polyarteritis Nodosa.
evidence.snippet: Polyarteritis nodosa is a rare disease characterized by the necrotizing inflammation of medium-sized arteries.
Polyarteritis nodosa (PAN) is a systemic necrotizing vasculitis predominantly targeting medium-sized arteries.
